name: Natalie
welcomeDialog: hiiii, I'm Natalie, what will it be for you today?? :)
fishInfo: hm...did you know monica, the pyjama shark loves the evening!! so cool right, she loves sleep time XD
giftReactionNEGATIVE: em...you're sweet but i don't really want anything from you

name: Travis
welcomeDialog: (robotically in moody voice) hello, thank you for your visit, do  you want a bag?
giftReactionNEGATIVE: sorry bro I don't take bribes

name: John
welcomeDialog: well hello there visitor, I hope you are enjoying the visit so far :)
fishInfo: hmm what could i tell you...oh! our local long-nosed friend juliet loves the sun, you almost only see her at noon
giftReactionPOSITIVE: oh thank you! you, my friend are a good person :)

name: Julia
welcomeDialog: hi! you need anything? sorry i'm quite busy over here but i will try my best to help :3
fishInfo: oh i can tell you about leo! hes the leopard shark, im usually here but in the morning i always come to say hi to him :3
giftReactionNEGATIVE: awww I'm so thankfull but I don't even have free hands to hold this, you should keep that for yourself

name: Michael
fishInfo: oh you ever went to see "the balloons" in the sea tank? they are shy little guys but when the sun comes down they come out
giftReactionPOSITIVE: is that a bagel?!! well that is a really nice surprise

name: Mariane
welcomeDialog:
hi I'm Mariane, I take care of octopuses here, well i take care of lots of fish here but my specialization are cephalopods. Squids, nautiluses and OCTOPUSES.
Did you know octopuses have blue blood? humans have haemoglobin in their blood cells but octopuses due to high copper level have haemocyanin instead which makes it bluish.
The reason for this is that haemocyanin wouldn't be efficient for humans but for sea creatures like octopuses it's actually better
because in conditions like low temperatures and high pressures it transports oxygen much faster.
Isn't that so cool?!
Oh sorry...I'm talking to much again right? :)
fishInfo:
YOU WANT TO KNOW SOMETHING ABOUT FISH???!!!! I CAN TELL YOU SOMETHING ABOUT OCTOPUSES!!!!
SO...
